content.claim: Finland's tiered support model (general, intensified, and special support) decouples formal SEN identification from access to educational support, with 31% of students receiving support at one tier or another but no requirement that children be labeled as having SEN to receive help
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: No comparative data on underlying identification or support need rates between Finland and diagnosis-gated systems
High support proportion (31%) raises question whether tiering reduces overidentification or simply relabels needs
No evidence on whether decoupling reduces stigmatization or ethnic/racial disparities in practice
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
